questions.q: Define RMS value of current and voltage.
questions.answer: The RMS (Root Mean Square) value of current and voltage is the square root of the mean value of the square of the current and voltage.
key_points: RMS value is used to measure AC quantities

questions.q: State the advantages of AC over DC.
questions.answer: Advantages of AC over DC are - easier transformation, efficient transmission, and simplicity of design.

questions.q: Explain the concept of frequency and its unit.
questions.answer: Frequency is the number of oscillations or cycles per second of a wave. Its unit is Hertz (Hz).
questions.topic: Time Period, Frequency and Angular Frequency

questions.answer: AC is a type of electric current that periodically reverses direction, whereas DC is a type of electric current that flows in one direction only.

questions.q: Derive the expression for RMS value of current and voltage.
questions.answer: Let i = I0 sin ωt be the instantaneous value of alternating current. Then, the RMS value is given by I = I0 / √2.

questions.q: Explain the principle of a transformer.
questions.answer: A transformer is an electrical device that transfers electrical energy from one circuit to another through electromagnetic induction. It consists of two coils: primary and secondary.

questions.q: Describe the construction and working of a step-down transformer.
questions.answer: A step-down transformer has a primary coil with a higher number of turns and a secondary coil with a lower number of turns. When an alternating current flows through the primary coil, a magnetic field is produced, inducing a voltage in the secondary coil.

questions.q: Prove that power in an AC circuit is given by P = VI cos φ.
questions.answer: Let i = I0 sin (ωt - φ) be the instantaneous current in the AC circuit. Then, the instantaneous power is given by p = VI cos φ.

questions.q: Explain the concept of impedance and its unit.
questions.answer: Impedance is the total opposition to the flow of an alternating current in a circuit. Its unit is ohm (Ω).

questions.q: Derive the expression for impedance of an AC circuit.
questions.answer: Let R be the resistance and XL be the inductive reactance in the AC circuit. Then, the impedance is given by Z = √(R2 + XL2).

questions.q: Describe the construction and working of a step-up transformer.
questions.answer: A step-up transformer has a primary coil with a lower number of turns and a secondary coil with a higher number of turns. When an alternating current flows through the primary coil, a magnetic field is produced, inducing a voltage in the secondary coil.

questions.q: Explain the concept of power factor and its significance.
questions.answer: Power factor is the ratio of the average power to the apparent power in an AC circuit. It is an important parameter that indicates the efficiency of the circuit.

questions.q: Determine the power factor of an AC circuit with a resistance of 10 Ω and an inductive reactance of 15 Ω.
questions.answer: The power factor is given by cos φ = R / Z = 10 / √(10^2 + 15^2) = 0.66.

questions.q: Explain the working of a wattmeter in an AC circuit.
questions.answer: A wattmeter is an instrument that measures the power consumed by an AC circuit. It consists of a moving coil and a fixed coil, and the deflection of the moving coil is proportional to the power consumed.
questions.topic: Measurement of Power in AC Circuits

questions.q: Describe the construction and working of a voltmeter in an AC circuit.
questions.answer: A voltmeter is an instrument that measures the potential difference across an AC circuit. It consists of a moving coil and a fixed coil, and the deflection of the moving coil is proportional to the potential difference.
questions.topic: Measurement of Power in AC Circuits

questions.q: Determine the current drawn by a circuit with a resistance of 20 Ω and an impedance of 25 Ω.
questions.answer: The current is given by I = V / Z = E / Z = 10 / 25 = 0.4 A.
